# Refactoring (Martin Fowler)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Refactoring: A First Example](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/01-refactoring-a-first-example.md): Code Refactoring, Legacy Code Modernization, Technical Debt Reduction, and Architectural Restructuring. This rule set activates when the user requests the AI to clean up, restructure, or refactor an existing function, separate calculation from rendering logic, or replace complex conditional switch statements with polymorphic class structures.
    - [Principles in Refactoring](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/02-principles-in-refactoring.md): Modifying existing code, adding new features to an established codebase, addressing technical debt, improving code readability, reorganizing system architecture, or specifically refactoring, cleaning up, or restructuring code.
    - [Bad Smells in Code](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/03-bad-smells-in-code.md): The domain defines rules that MUST be activated whenever the AI is tasked with reading, analyzing, reviewing, or refactoring existing source code, specifically during tasks such as 'code cleanup,' 'technical debt reduction,' 'improving readability,' 'code review,' or explicit 'refactoring.'
    - [Building Tests](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/04-building-tests.md): The domain encompasses tasks related to writing, modifying, or analyzing tests, setting up testing frameworks, refactoring code that requires test coverage, and fixing reported bugs in a codebase.
    - [Introducing the Catalog](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/05-introducing-the-catalog.md): Documenting, defining, proposing, or executing code refactorings; creating a refactoring catalog; structuring educational or reference material about code transformations.
    - [A First Set of Refactorings](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/06-a-first-set-of-refactorings.md): Code refactoring, readability enhancements, modularization, variable scope management, API modification, and logic decomposition tasks.
    - [Encapsulation](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/07-encapsulation.md): These rules are activated when the AI performs refactoring tasks aimed at improving information hiding, data protection, and module decomposition. This includes manipulating data structures, collections, primitive values, temporary variables, class boundaries, and object delegations.
    - [Moving Features](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/08-moving-features.md): Restructuring code layout, moving functions or fields between classes/modules, consolidating duplicated execution logic, reordering statements for clarity, converting iterative loops to collection pipelines, or cleaning up unused/deprecated code.
    - [Organizing Data](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/09-organizing-data.md): Triggered when the user requests code cleanup, refactoring, or restructuring specifically related to data structures, variable declarations, field naming, mutability, object identity (values vs. references), and state management.
    - [Simplifying Conditional Logic](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/10-simplifying-conditional-logic.md): Refactoring, analyzing, or writing code that involves conditional logic, control flow operations (if/else, switch), repeated checks for specific values (e.g., null or "unknown"), deeply nested branching, or implicit state assumptions.
    - [Refactoring APIs](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/11-refactoring-apis.md): Designing, modifying, or refactoring module boundaries, class interfaces, function signatures, or application programming interfaces (APIs), specifically involving tasks such as adding parameters, changing method visibility, splitting logic, or managing object instantiation and state mutation.
    - [Dealing with Inheritance](.roo/vendor/architecture/refactoring-martin-fowler/12-dealing-with-inheritance.md): Creating, modifying, or reviewing object-oriented class hierarchies, managing inheritance structures, dealing with type-code-based conditional logic, or resolving tight coupling between parent and child classes.
